content: I’ve been very hyped for this because I’ve wanted (and tried) to play DnD for awhile now, so I’m pretty sure I’m the only one who has been putting this much thought into the character at this point. A lot of this has also been me thinking alone, so a lot will depend on what the dm thinks of all of this
I'm glad you're giving it a try, it's a lot of fun. Is it specifically a limbus company themed adventure?
nah, but me and another friend just like limbus alot
i was having trouble deciding on my subclass and all that so i figured i would jsut take some of my favorite characters and do something based on them

Gotcha. I can see the temptation and if your heart is set on being Vespa I would encourage it, but I'm going to be honest in saying I've seen a lot of people make external characters in D&D and it never quite works as well as they'd think, or they end up having to put in more work just to BE that character rather than become their own thing. What makes D&D special is that you can make your own character and experience their development through gameplay
yeah, ive only really thought about the characters for things like skills and stats and all that, I haven't taken the story or actual characterization from any limbus characters

Making a character that performs well mechanically is a small fraction of the fun of D&D so I wouldn't sweat it too much, I'd probably just recommend sticking to one class to ease the learning curve. Hexblade Warlock is one of the better classes in the game and is frankly, cool as fuck.

this was actually my second character idea, and i scrapped the first one for this reason. I was going to make an Astral Hand Monk, and he was based on a Middle member who was basically ripped from Limbus, and I thought that would be really cool until I realised that he would be super Wis and Con heavy with charisma as his dump stat, and that it didn't really align with who I am or who the character was
yeah, I think i'm just gonna run the hexblade warlock because I can recall my weapon like I wanted, plus i get pretty crazy abilities like eldritch blast
I definitely want to run a high charisma character because I like to be funny, and I don't think I want to be a super stoic character or a really dumb character or anything

That's always tough when the stats just aren't lining up. Charisma is fun. I will say though, even if you don't have charisma you don't have to be stoic. Dump stat charisma could just mean always choosing the worst dialogue option in every conversation, which can be quite funny
